meaning: n.<日>榻榻米(日本人铺在室内地板上的稻草垫)
examples: His hand rested on the tatami by his side.
Would you like to sit in a "tatami" room or at a table?
The tatami room is designed contrastingly in black and silver.
The goods concerned-long-stem onions, shiitake mushrooms, and tatami rushes-accounted for less than 0.2 per cent of bilateral trade.
From then on, Schwartz, who became chairman, did his best to sweep the incident under the tatami mat.
